entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) susceptible to downy mildew.

entities.name: DOWNY MILDEW
entities.description: A disease also known as green ear, caused by Sclerospora graminicola, which causes severe loss in grain yield.

entities.name: SCLEROSPORA GRAMINICOLA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes downy mildew in pearl millet.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced, which is reduced by the conversion of florets into leafy structures due to disease.

entities.name: HOST-PLANT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The most feasible method to control downy mildew disease.

entities.name: GENETIC STOCKS
entities.description: Lines developed from spontaneously occurring mutants used for pearl millet improvement.

entities.name: MUTANTS
entities.description: Spontaneously occurring variants found in the world collection of pearl millet germplasm.

entities.name: WORLD COLLECTION OF PEARL MILLET GERMPLASM
entities.description: A global repository of pearl millet genetic resources from which mutants were identified.

entities.name: MORPHOLOGICAL CHARACTERS
entities.description: Physical characteristics used to describe the developed genetic stocks.

entities.name: AGRONOMICAL CHARACTERS
entities.description: Agricultural performance characteristics used to describe the developed genetic stocks.

entities.name: SCREENING
entities.description: The process of evaluating genetic stocks for resistance to downy mildew.

relationships.target: WORLD COLLECTION OF PEARL MILLET GERMPLASM
